rationale: The pair is likely more than an incidental mention because the citing paper’s core question is when consumption regulations/warnings backfire, including an unhealthy-food warning-label experiment, while the cited paper is directly about a labeling backfire effect (“Can ‘Low-Fat’ Nutrition Labels Lead to Obesity?”). The cited note reports mechanism-based evidence (serving-size inferences, guilt) and policy-relevant findings on label-induced overconsumption, which could plausibly serve as a foundational prior result in this domain. With this topical and conceptual overlap, it should be retained for full relation review.
